mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.5% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2019-01-15 indicates deep easing cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 1.5% do not significantly exceed baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market strength that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained employment growth
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that portends earnings downgrades and equity multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month by > 2% in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 424,481 million and HOUST at 836 thousand as of 2019-01-15
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint decline) shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declining below 98.5 signals supply-side weakness and capacity utilization stress that constrains profit margins and growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.9462 as of 2019-01-15, near trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.4% as of 2019-01-15 is well above trigger, but this reflects data lag
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 52% of periods when UNRATE > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint signal of low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 1.0%) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month > 0.3%) creates financial repression conditions that boost equity valuations as real yields compress, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL at 233.318 as of 2019-01-15
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL growth) shows no incremental explanatory power beyond individual series (F-test p > 0.10), or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of co-triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) expanding above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports earnings growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate exceeds 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15,648.7 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2018-10-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 58% of periods with GDPC1 growth > 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 800 thousand units signals residential demand weakness that predicts broader economic slowdown and equity earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 836 thousand as of 2019-01-15, near trigger threshold
regime_hint: housing market slowdown or demand-side contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
